name: Tour of Alto Palancia — Circular from Segorbe through Montmayor
name_original: Vuelta por el Alto Palancia — Circular desde Segorbe por Montmayor
region_intro: Road cycling around Segorbe offers diverse terrain situated between the Sierra Calderona and Sierra de Espadán Natural Parks. The region features well-maintained asphalt roads through dense pine forests and cork oak groves, with gradients often less than 5% in Sierra Calderona. More challenging routes with gradients up to 10% are found in Sierra de Espadán. The Palancia River valley contributes picturesque views with olive, almond, and cherry trees. Best road cycling routes around Segorbe The most popular road cycling route is Natural Park of Serra d'Espada — Circular from Segorbe to Aín, a 33.5 miles (54.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 44 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the rugged landscapes of the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Tour of Alto Palancia — Circular from Segorbe through Montmayor, a difficult 58.8 miles (94.6 km) path. This route traverses the scenic Palancia River valley, offering views of agricultural landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the L'Oronet Pass Climb – Oronet Pass loop from Segorbe - Ciudad, a 50.0 miles (80.4 km) trail leading through the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, often completed in about 5 hours 21 minutes. Road cycling around Segorbe is defined by Mediterranean forests, river valleys, and iconic mountain passes like L'Oronet. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate gradients to challenging climbs. The routes in Segorbe are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 2500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Segorbe's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Segorbe?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes around Segorbe, catering to various skill levels. You'll find 2 easy routes, 14 moderate routes, and 25 challenging options for experienced cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Segorbe?
A: Road cycling around Segorbe offers diverse terrain. You'll find well-maintained asphalt roads through dense pine and cork oak forests in the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, often with moderate gradients under 5%. The Sierra de Espadán Natural Park presents more challenging routes with gradients up to 10%. The Palancia River valley provides picturesque views of olive, almond, and cherry trees.

Q: Are there any challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists in the Segorbe area?
A: Yes, Segorbe is an excellent destination for challenging climbs. The Sierra de Espadán Natural Park features routes with gradients up to 10%. Iconic climbs like El Garbí and L'Oronet Pass within the Sierra Calderona also offer significant elevation gain and spectacular panoramic views. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39566546" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Oronet Pass Climb – Oronet Pass loop from Segorbe - Ciudad&lt;/a&gt; for a demanding ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Segorbe?
A: Segorbe enjoys a Mediterranean climate with over 300 sunny days annually, making it a year-round destination for road cycling. Spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures, while summer can be warm but still enjoyable, especially during early mornings or late afternoons. Winters are generally mild.

Q: Are there any family-friendly or easy road cycling options near Segorbe?
A: While many routes are challenging, the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, Spain's longest Greenway, passes close to Segorbe. This car-free cycling path is predominantly downhill when traveled from Teruel towards the Valencian coast, making it accessible and enjoyable for families and cyclists of varying fitness levels.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes around Segorbe offer stunning natural features and viewpoints. You can expect dense pine and cork oak forests, picturesque river valleys, and red sandstone cliffs. Iconic mountain passes like L'Oronet and El Garbí provide spectacular panoramic views, often including glimpses of the Mediterranean Sea on clear days. You might also encounter natural enclaves such as the Fountain and Chapel of Hope or the Fountain of the 50 pipes.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Segorbe?
A: The road cycling routes in Segorbe are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the well-maintained and quiet roads, and the stunning natural beauty of the Sierra Calderona and Sierra de Espadán Natural Parks.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available from Segorbe?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Segorbe are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e881818602/natural-park-of-serra-d-espada-circular-from-segorbe-to-ain" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of Serra d'Espada — Circular from Segorbe to Aín&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e883438235/tour-of-alto-palancia-circular-from-segorbe-through-montmayor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Alto Palancia — Circular from Segorbe through Montmayor&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or attractions to explore near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Segorbe's charming medieval old town offers a cultural backdrop with its castle ruins, cathedral, and ancient city walls. For natural landmarks, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1054060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garbí Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or exploring the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2715232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail Along Barranco Codobal&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some of the longer road cycling routes around Segorbe?
A: For longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e883438235/tour-of-alto-palancia-circular-from-segorbe-through-montmayor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Alto Palancia — Circular from Segorbe through Montmayor&lt;/a&gt; covers nearly 95 kilometers. Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39566546" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Oronet Pass Climb – Oronet Pass loop from Segorbe - Ciudad&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 80 kilometers long.

Q: Are the roads generally safe and low-traffic for road cycling in Segorbe?
A: Yes, the region is known for its smooth, quiet roads with low vehicle traffic, especially within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park. This contributes to a peaceful and enjoyable cycling experience, making many routes feel safe and less crowded.

Q: Can I find routes that explore both the Sierra Calderona and Sierra de Espadán Natural Parks?
A: Many routes are strategically located to allow exploration of both natural parks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e881818602/natural-park-of-serra-d-espada-circular-from-segorbe-to-ain" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of Serra d'Espada — Circular from Segorbe to Aín&lt;/a&gt; specifically delves into the rugged landscapes of Sierra de Espadán, while other routes like the L'Oronet Pass loop traverse the Sierra Calderona, offering a diverse cycling experience.
tips: One of the longest ports in Castellón. Quite harsh in the summer. There is hardly any shade or refill points (when we went in August, the bar and bathrooms in Cueva Santa were closed and the fountain was not working). The unevenness is constant.
